scope.summary: Implement a `PinConstraint` (per-particle positional lock, inv_mass = 0) and a `FreezeRegion` operator/model command that pins a named set of particles for the duration of a simulation run or until explicitly unpinned, mirroring MD's Pin and Freeze interactive tools
acceptance_criteria: `GarmentSpec` gains `pin_regions: Vec<PinRegionSpec>` where `PinRegionSpec` carries `panel_id: String`, `vertex_selector: PinSelector` (enum: `BoundaryAll`, `VertexIds(Vec<u32>)`, `UvRegion { u_min, u_max, v_min, v_max }`), and `unpin_at_frame: Option<u32>`

At `SolverMesh` build time, pinned vertices receive `GpuParticle.inv_mass = 0.0`; the WGSL `integrate.wgsl` shader skips force integration for particles with `inv_mass == 0.0` (already defined in the spec struct comment at line 1474)

When `unpin_at_frame` is set, the animation loop (MT-289) uploads an updated particle buffer at that frame restoring the original `inv_mass` from the fabric density; the event `TailorPinRegionReleased` is emitted to the EventLedger

An MCP tool `pin_region` is exposed (parallel to `place_trim`) accepting `garment_id`, `panel_id`, and `vertex_selector`; models can pin a garment shoulder panel, run simulation, and observe the rest of the garment draping while the pinned region is held
Integration test: pin the waistband of a skirt, run a drape sim, assert pinned vertices have displacement < 0.1 mm and the rest of the skirt drapes normally (NO_INTERPENETRATION passes)
scope.risk_if_missed: Operators and models cannot hold garment regions in place during interactive draping or animation; MD-style fitting workflows (hold collar while draping bodice, freeze pinned hem while re-simming below) are impossible. The refit pipeline (MT-299-MT-316) also lacks a pin-assist for stable garment transfer.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
